title: Continuous Ingestion, Reindexing, Cutover, and Disaster Recovery · Behavior Case: Rag Backup Restore Rebuild
summary: A nightly job rebuilds the live index in place, loses failed documents, publishes partial results, misses deletes, and has backups that were never restored. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the backup restore rebuild increment in recoverable continuous-indexing system. Learners progress through prediction, ex…
objectives: Choose what to back up versus rebuild, retain source and configuration identity, test restore objectives, and verify retrieval and deletion after recovery.
Reconcile source, metadata, chunks, vectors, lexical index, graph, caches, and tombstones and prove bounded visibility lag.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-backup-restore-rebuild — Choose what to back up versus rebuild, retain source and configuration identity, test restore objectives, and verify retrieval and deletion after recovery.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-ingestion-operations-recovery-5
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-ingestion-operations-recovery-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def worked_rag_ingestion_operations_recovery_5(source_version: int = 14, indexed_version: int = 14, failed_documents: int = 0, dead_letters: int = 0, blue_green_ready: bool = True, restore_verified: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "query5dekrow",
        "tenant9036",
        "indexworked59036",
        "prompt5dekrow9036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if source_version < 1 or indexed_version < 1:
        return False, 0, "version-input"
    if indexed_version != source_version:
        return False, indexed_version, "index-lag"
    if failed_documents != dead_letters:
        return False, failed_documents, "failure-accounting"
    if not blue_green_ready:
        return False, indexed_version, "unsafe-cutover"
    if not restore_verified:
        return False, indexed_version, "unproven-recovery"
    assert failed_documents == dead_letters
    return True, indexed_version, "recoverable-index"

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose what to back up versus rebuild, retain source and configuration identity, test restore objectives, and verify retrieval and deletion after recovery. Artifact under review: recoverable continuous-indexing system.

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Backup Restore Rebuild model · Theory Rag Backup Restore Rebuild
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Backup Restore Rebuild: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 1333 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to operate change capture, retries, dead letters, reindexing, blue-green cutover, deletion, and restore; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose what to back up versus rebuild, retain source and configuration identity, test restore objectives, and verify retrieval and deletion after recovery.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Backup Restore Rebuild model · Theory Rag Backup Restore Rebuild.
steps.why: Retrieving Rag Backup Restore Rebuild through the concrete recoverable continuous-indexing system case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Backup Restore Rebuild model · Theory Rag Backup Restore Rebuild, keep Rag Backup Restore Rebuild connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose what to back up versus rebuild, retain source and configuration identity, test restore objectives, and verify retrieval and deletion after recovery.
